title: Request: Updates Editable by Anyone
excerpt:
Hey @monday-team, we are a marketing agency currently in the process of migrating from Wrike to Monday. 
The main suggestion that we have, and one that is vital to our company’s success on Monday, is the ability for anyo…

posts.author: zoelloydd (Zoe Lloydd)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @monday-team, we are a marketing agency currently in the process of migrating from Wrike to Monday.

The main suggestion that we have, and one that is vital to our company’s success on Monday, is the ability for anyone to edit updates (not just the person who created the update) - We have found that most other PM platforms allow this and it is a significant issue for migrating our projects to Monday as well as the services that we want to offer clients.

We have gone through multiple meetings and scoping sessions with our Monday partners to explore other options, however Monday is currently lacking the feature of an editable description box or updates section by more than one person.

As you can see from the screenshot attached, only the creator of the update has access to editing it - meaning no other employees or clients are able to. We would love to see the implementation of an updates section or description box that is editable by anyone.

Screen Shot 2020-08-25 at 1.27.59 PM1174×1374 189 KB

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there @zoelloydd - this sounds like a great custom feature! Would love to learn more and see if our developers can help you do more with monday.

You can book a time to chat here: Book a call with Polished Geek: Do more with monday․com

Looking forward to our conversation.
~ Deb

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
zoelloydd:

Monday is currently lacking the feature of an editable description box or updates section by more than one person.

Agree. This is a significant design flaw in the Update feature in Monday.

Multi-user updating editable by anyone is required in our work environment too.

We have had to accept that the only way to edit an update is to add another update to the item posting a correction.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 on this one. This was request via support too.

posts.author: sschreiner (Sebastian Schreiner)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 - We can’t really build shared checklists as long as this isn’t an option. Requiring the creator of the update to be the only one who can modify an update and therefore a checklist introduces unnecessary bottlenecks. At least allow others to modify the update if the creator allows it - similar to the info boxes.

posts.author: justin.xlsx (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Guys, is there an update on this? Really useful for our agency to collaborate better!

posts.author: Shauna_UNBC (Shauna)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday.com please add this feature for updates editable by anyone. It would really help with collaborating.

posts.author: aaragon (Amanda A)
posts.cookedHtml: We are struggling with this limitation right now. We created checklist items and no one else can edit the checklist to add or remove items as needed. It’s a huge flaw. We definitely need this feature.

posts.author: gramb0t (Graham N.)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 in 2022.
At least maybe allow board owners to update updates on boards they own. having updates on updates to correct an update is a crummy user experience.

posts.author: sjerger (Sarah Jerger)
posts.cookedHtml: Another vote for this feature to be added. Creates a lot of unnecessary work.

posts.author: Nizo (Moe Nizo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello dear,
Is there any update on this? Just give everyone right to work on the same Note (no need to call it an update)
And if someone wants to lock their note they might want to have the option to.
Please update the software soon🙏 many other platforms are not having this problem, but we want to stick with Monday.com

posts.author: sdubowik (Sergej)
posts.cookedHtml: need it. not having this feature… leads to too much copy-paste work… at the moment

posts.author: ll4mat (Knecht Rootrecht)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 here.

We would also be happy about such a function because we often use checklists in certain boards (updates) which partly have to be adapted by other board members.

Currently the boardmember who wants to change something in such a checklist has to copy it every time and post it again in a new update. This usually ends up in the fact that in an item 3 different checklists are posted in 3 separate updates by 3 separate users and in each case only one or two points where adjusted.

I probably don’t have to mention that the clarity goes down the drain when working like this.

posts.author: anthonywoods (Anthony Woods)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 here

We are in a similiar situation where we have a team that are required to update existing updates in order to tick off items within a check list. However, the person who creates the update with the checklist, is the only person who can amend it.

I hope Monday.com can look into this and either

a) Allow everyone to edit each other’s updates with a recorded record
or
b) Allow the person who posts the update, to choose which user can edit their update.

posts.author: Jorge (Jorge)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe at least checklists are not editable. Is there any workaround other than copy and paste?

posts.author: allyssa (Lyssa)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
Trying out Monday and can’t believe this is not a thing!! Or at least add a text field with formatting available. Is everyone just copy and pasting rn?

posts.author: OfficialTahaAbbasi (Taha Abbasi)
posts.cookedHtml: One option can be to make a Description section for each item. Similar to Jira. Where a Description can be added which is editable by anyone who has edit permission on the baord, this way user’s can add things like checklists in Descriptions, and the updates can remain uneditable.

posts.author: CarolChiang (Carol Chiang)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1
Really need this feature especially creating a checklist for my department

posts.author: karkib1 (Bipin Karki)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is very essential. Would love to see @monday.com implementing this soon.

posts.author: steeleangel (Jessica Angel)
posts.cookedHtml: I came looking for a solution to allow team members to edit updates, and I was disappointed to find it is not a feature. This is a necessary feature. I hope Monday.com implements this update soon.

posts.author: Lucas2 (Lucas Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml: I made an automation to write updates, and even I can’t edit it because it was made by automations?? But I can delete any update I want? Nice. People have been begging for this for 3 years now monday.com.

posts.author: skosi (Stefan Kosarnig)
posts.cookedHtml: +1’000 We can’t really build shared checklists as long as this isn’t an option. Requiring the creator of the update to be the only one who can modify an update and therefore a checklist introduces unnecessary bottlenecks. At least allow others to modify the update if the creator allows it - similar to the info boxes.

posts.author: BrianPuccio (Brian Puccio)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am trying (with little success) to use Monday as a ticketing/task tracking system largely for technical deliverables (think SNow, Jira, GitHub, GitLab, etc.). A key feature of these systems is tickets have both a title and a body where more information can be provided.

I’ve worked around Monday’s lack of a “ticket body” by pinning a comment on each ticket I open. However, because I’m not working alone but in a team, it is crucial that teammates can update “ticket bodies” over the life of a ticket so we can always tell what each ticket is about and its state.

Inability for teammates to update “ticket bodies” is antithetical to every ticketing application I’ve used.

This request has been open for over three years – has anyone else found a suitable workaround to make Monday work more like ticketing software?

Thanks!

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml: There is the option of using ‘Info Boxes’ which is one of the sections within an update, and can be edited by anyone… but they don’t display as updates on the update icon on the item/subitem, so unless you know they’re there or where to look, it doesn’t really work as an ‘update’… especially as I don’t believe that there is any automation that can be triggered by updates to them if desired for notifications or actual updates.

posts.author: ScottMuirZapi (Scott Muir)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1

Has anyone found a solution to this?

It is a critical function to making the updates feature truly useable in a team

posts.author: dstein (Dennis Stein)
posts.cookedHtml: I also desperately need to be able to allow others to edit my updates. The “Info Box” workaround doesn’t work (I tried giving ownership to a user and they still couldn’t edit it). Info Boxes are also hidden on a different tab and wouldn’t be noticed by many users. Any updates on allowing another user to edit my updates? Thank you!

posts.author: tommemben (Tom Charde)
posts.cookedHtml:
So, we were able to edit other users’ updates from about 2022-2023 (maybe we were part of a secret test group?), but we all just realized that this ability has recently been removed.

This is essential for anyone using Monday for project management. Without it, our process is now logjammed until we figure out an alternative solution.

posts.author: alyssah (Alyssa Hatfield)
posts.cookedHtml: +1! Our team would greatly benefit from this added feature. In one instance, my team plans for a weekly newsletter and in our final stages one person creates a “DRAFT” update for us to review. It would be much more convenient for different team members to make edits/add content to the update itself rather than requesting the edits be made by the original poster.

posts.author: andrewc02 (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a team member in a scenario with multiple “board owners”, it would be extremely beneficial to allow Updates to be edited by other “board owners”.
Currently, this capability is limited to the creator of the Update, while “board owners” can Delete an Update but cannot edit it.
After deletion, they can proceed to create an Update of their own.
In many cases for our team, the text of the original Update may only require minor adjustments, such as adding the name of a client or @board guest, as the board has been created from a template providing the basic structure. Such edits can involve tailoring content to specific clients or simply personalising the Update.

image1556×707 132 KB

posts.author: Joe_Downey (Joe Downey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday is a fantastic tool, but I find the lack of transparency and interaction by the Monday.com team around certain features very off putting. This has been asked for since 2020, with recent activity.

Can we please get an update on this. If it’s not going to happen, then please say so. If it can’t happen, just explain why.

Thank you

@monday-moderators @monday-team @CSG

posts.author: Johnny (Johnny Morse)
posts.cookedHtml: • 1 to this. This and being able to create a permissions view/edit layer to individual updates would really help streamline client communications. We really struggle with these restrictions and they limit our ability to use Monday for client services.

posts.author: JanL (Jan Lidtke)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, this seems to be requested quite a bit. We update our tasks all the time and it gets super messy if each tiny change is a separate update

Layer 1688×1176 55.6 KB

posts.author: DylanDowns (Dylan Downs)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve solved this in our organisation by adding ‘Description’ module to the item updates view. This is a collaborative rich text tab that everyone can edit.

image609×687 16.8 KB

posts.author: Renae-JTsCloud (Renae-upstream)
posts.cookedHtml: My team has discovered a native way to work around this- we are going to be running a short (20mins) workshop next week for our clients to show them how they can get around this. If you’d like an invite, send me an email at renae@upstreamtech.io and I can add you onto the invite list so you can solve!

posts.author: pkk (PK)
posts.cookedHtml:
Item description seems promising, but would like the ability to link the description across multiple items and use a template. Essentially a Monday doc but less buggy. The ability to link is useful if an item needs to be split up across multiple items.

We went with a Monday doc on the item, but have run into so many issues that it is time for us to find a different solution. One of the main issues being not being able to access the doc.

posts.author: pjtgrizzly (Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you share in here what is a native workaround on this?

posts.author: Steddy (Matthew Stedman)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have >50 tasks…each represents a deliverable that may have 1 or many issues we track in a differnt JRA system that may be holding up acceptance (Task completion). Checklists is (currently) the most efficent way to track status for ths type of thing in Monday.com : Especially as the web version displays a checked/unchecked count of checklist items next to each task name.

However as checklists can only exist in an update I create… other team members cannot add additional checklist items.

I create a common update contain ing 1 checklist and pin it so it remains first.

The wish is that task owners in my team can then go about populating additional checklist items (1 for each defect JIRAL URL) and keep the status uptodate

Our exec can then quickly see movement towards task completion.

One Monday.com task may have 20 or more defects..checklist items.

My Request : Can Monday.com please create a new privilage, which if associated to a user, allows that user to edit an updates within tasks created by other users.

I am an enterpirse user and am interested in understandign how I could get this prioritised.

posts.author: Josh (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have updates that list client monthly deliverables and want to be able to have multiple users make edits to these updates.

For examole account managers may meet with clients and need to make updates to the initial strategy tasks listed by the account strategy manager. Then a technical team member may need to add hyper links as proof of work when checking off listed tasks.

Need this ASAP if possible.

posts.author: gogreg (Greg Abaoag)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday.com any update here? Haven’t seen any progress or maybe close it if its already resolved. Thanks.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Matthew! Shannon here with the monday team. Thank you for submitting your feature request this past October. This feature is not yet on the product roadmap, but I am merging your post with this request here to ensure that votes are accurately counted. Before merging, your request had 1 vote. The combined total is now 60 votes!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Josh! Thanks for submitting your feature request this past October. I want to ensure that votes are accurately counted for your feature request, so I’ve merged your submission with this request here. Before merging, your request had 1 vote. The combined total is now 61 votes!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for following up on this feature request, Greg! This feature is not yet on the product roadmap, but I’ve merged Josh’s request with this one here to centralize all of the votes in one place! You can refer to this thread in the future for any updates from our Product team.

posts.author: ScottLP (Scott)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Shannon. Has there been any update on this? It’s something we need to be able to consolidate Checklist items. We have been waiting for this for a few years
